mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive return frequency < 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the trigger activates.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly declines in real GDP and monthly retail sales indicate a synchronized collapse in aggregate demand, which compresses corporate earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal yields a positive return frequency above 50% or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency falls below 45% during confirmed expansion phases or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter future monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered months or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market weakness rather than seasonal adjustment, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns with a frequency above 50% or yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over 3-year rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which stimulates downstream economic growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency drops below 48% during housing starts expansions or Rank IC remains below 0.015 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production confirm a sustained manufacturing contraction, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following the trigger or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.
